Are there any charges for adding my bank account to Scripbox?
Modified on Wed, 13 Nov, 2024 at  5:41 PM
Adding your bank account to Scripbox is completely free of charge. However, your bank may levy a nominal one-time fee for linking your bank account to Scripbox. This fee is determined by the bank.
Bank linking via Net Banking – You may be charged between Rs.2 to Rs.5
Bank linking via paper form – Varies across banks
We recommend that you check with your bank regarding any fee for linking
your bank account as policies can keep changing.

Sorry! nothing found for
What FATCA-related information will you collect?
Modified on Thu, 14 Nov, 2024 at 12:10 PM
We are required to collect the following information from you:
For Resident Indians:
Country of birth
Source of income ( eg – salary/business etc.)
For NRIs/PIOs:
Names of Countries where you pay tax
The tax identification numbers for those countries
NRI clients must provide TIN numbers when declaring FATCA.
In situations where no TIN is available (e.g., in Arab countries), one of the following alternatives is required:
Social Security Number (SSN) from the country of residence
Citizen Reference Number (similar to Aadhaar)
Residential Identification Number or National Insurance ID
Sea-farer’s Identification Number
Please find the attached document for TIN Structure File
Attachments (1)
pdf
NRI - TIN FORMAT - Sheet1.pdf
40 KB

title: I’m already a Mutual Fund investor. Do I need to do KYC again?
meta_description: We will only verify your KYC status from the central repository to ensure that you are KYC compliant. It is a one time activity and takes only a few minutes
content:
All
Articles
Recent Searches
Clear all
No recent searches
Popular Articles
Articles
View all
Topics
View all
Tickets
View all
Sorry! nothing found for
I’m already a Mutual Fund investor. Do I need to do KYC again?
Modified on Wed, 13 Nov, 2024 at  7:08 PM
No. We will only verify your KYC status from the central repository to ensure that you are KYC compliant. It is a one-time activity and takes only a few minutes. Please complete your signup and get started with your investments. As per recent regulation by SEBI, the investors' KYC is required to be validated by Aadhar to invest in Mutual funds.
Whether you need to do KYC again depends on your current KYC status:
KYC Validated:
No action is required.
KYC Registered or Verified:
You can continue to make transactions in your existing mutual funds, but you are required to validate your KYC with Aadhar to invest in new schemes post 1st April 2024.
KYC On-Hold:
You can't make any transactions until you redo KYC.
You can also redo KYC registration using PAN and Aadhaar through XML, Digi-locker, or m-aadhaar. If you are registered with a driving licence, voter ID, or passport, you are required to validate your KYC with Aadhaar to be able to invest in any mutual fund.

title: Why is KYC required to invest in Mutual Funds and How is it done?
meta_description: Some of these requirements are specified by SEBI (Securities and Exchange Board of India). Others have been included by us in order to simplify the registration process for the majority of investors.
content:
All
Articles
Recent Searches
Clear all

View all
Tickets
View all
Sorry! nothing found for
Why is KYC required to invest in Mutual Funds and How is it done?
Modified on Wed, 13 Nov, 2024 at  7:13 PM
KYC (Know Your Customer)
is required for investing in mutual funds to establish an investor's identity and address, and to prevent money laundering and other criminal activities:
Purpose
KYC is required by the Prevention of Money Laundering Act, of 2002, and is a part of the broader anti-money laundering and counter terrorism financing regulations.
Process
To complete KYC, you need to submit supporting documents such as a PAN card, address proof, and in-person verification (IPV). You can complete KYC online or offline.
Please note:- Scripbox will no longer be processing any fresh KYC verification requests on our platform for new customers.

Yes, nominee declaration is mandatory. If you wish not to add a nominee, you can select “Don’t want to add nominee” in the registration process.
However, we highly recommend that you add a nominee. The nomination facility has been designed to ensure smooth transfer of an investor’s wealth, should something happen to the investor.

How can I set up my Scripbox investment account?
Modified on Thu, 14 Nov, 2024 at 12:32 PM
You can setup your Scripbox investment account by following few simple steps -
Get started by verifying your email with an OTP
Enter your PAN and Date of Birth
We take your consent to fetch your KYC details and then proceed further accordingly (If the KYC is registered and validated by Aadhar, you will be directed to the next page and if your KYC is not registered then you will directed to a page where you can get your KYC registered, post which you can resume your sign up with Scripbox
Enter a few details required by compliance related to your income
Enter you bank account and we will verify it for you
Add a nominee to your investments
That’s it, you are all set to start investing! Sign up now.

title: Can NRIs Open a Mutual Funds Investment Account with Scripbox?
meta_description: Yes, NRI customers are supported on Scripbox. You can open your investment account and start investing in mutual funds.
content:
All
Articles
Recent Searches
Clear all
No recent searches
Popular Articles
Articles
View all
Topics
View all
Tickets
View all
Sorry! nothing found for
Can NRIs Open a Mutual Funds Investment Account with Scripbox?
Modified on Wed, 13 Nov, 2024 at  7:29 PM
As an NRI, the possibility of opening a Mutual Funds investment account with Scripbox depends on your citizenship and whether you hold an OCI (Overseas Citizenship of India) card.
OCI Cardholders
(Persons of Indian Origin/PIOs): As of May 2024, Scripbox does permit PIOs to open accounts. However, you will need to upload your OCI card during the signup process as part of the mandatory verification procedure.
US/Canada Citizens
: Unfortunately, Scripbox does not currently allow new account signups for individuals residing in the US or Canada. This restriction is in place regardless of whether you are a new investor or an existing Scripbox customer transitioning to NRI status.
Exceptions:
Individuals from restricted countries specified in SEBI regulations and circulars are strictly prohibited from investing in Indian markets. Consequently, if you are from one of these countries, you would be unable to open an account with Scripbox.

How do I add an investor, my child, to my Scripbox investment account?
Modified on Thu, 29 Jun, 2023 at  3:45 PM
You can easily create a Scripbox account for any family member by clicking on "Add an Investor" within your Scripbox account. When adding a child, please note the following:
Bank Account Details: Provide the bank account details that are in your child's name.
Same Guardian: The guardian of the minor's bank account and the guardian of the minor's Scripbox account must be the same person.
Joint Bank Account: If you are adding the details of a joint bank account, ensure that your child is one of the bank account holders, preferably the primary account holder.

Why do I need to invest using my child’s bank account for my child’s investments?
Modified on Thu, 29 Jun, 2023 at  3:46 PM
According to SEBI regulations, a guardian can invest in a minor's account only using the minor's bank account. Additionally, please keep the following points in mind:
The guardian of the minor's bank account and the guardian of the minor's Scripbox account must be the same person
If you are adding details of a joint bank account, ensure that your child is one of the bank account holders, preferably the primary account holder

title: How do I modify my KYC?
content:
All

View all
Sorry! nothing found for
How do I modify my KYC?
Modified on Tue, 28 Oct, 2025 at 12:59 PM
Please find the below scenarios where your KYC is required to be modified.
1. If the KYC status is on hold or rejected, the KYC needs to be modified with Aadhar.
2. If the KYC is registered with DL, Voter ID or Passport, It is advised to complete the KYC online by submitting Aadhaar as OVD proof, and giving a valid email id and mobile so that you can invest with any mutual funds in future.
In case it is not an Aadhaar-based KYC, you will have to submit the KYC documents every time you approach a new Mutual Fund for investment.
3.
If the KYC is registered with Non-OVD documents like Bank Statement, Landline bill, Electricity Bill etc, then the KYC will have to be modified with Aadhar.
You can get your KYC modified online with the respective KRA, where your KYC is registered. Kindly use this link:
https://www.cvlkra.com/
to check the KYC status.
You also have an option to modify the KYC online using the links below:
1.
KYC modification online via DSP
2.
KYC modification online via Nippon
These links allow you to modify your KYC by submitting your Aadhaar, PAN using the digilocker facility. It hardly takes 10 minutes to complete the entire process.
OR
We can also help you to get your KYC modified, for which you must share the physical copies of the documents below:
1. KYC form: Kindly affix your passport-size photo, sign across the same, and sign on the first and second page in the given slot (Applicant E sign and Applicant Wet signature). The  contact number, email ID, and PAN details have to be filled in. Please do not fill in any other details, we will do the needful as per the documents submitted to us. Kindly find the attached KYC form below.
2. Self-attested and Notarised copy of PAN
3. Self-attested and Notarised copy of Aadhar card (the document should be in colour copy)
Please note : A notarized copy is being insisted as currently we don't have the functionality to provide an e-sign. Notarization is required in lieu of the original seen and verified requirement of KRA for updating any changes in KYC.
Since we are not verifying the originals (being a digital platform), hence, we are insisting on notarization.
Note
: We request you to share the clear scanned documents for verification via email or on our whats app number i.e. 8884448026. Post verification you can courier us the documents to the below address.
(Scripbox Wealth Managers Pvt Ltd.  Wilshire III by MFAR, 5th Floor, 492, Hobli, RHB Colony, Mahadevapura, Bengaluru, Karnataka 560048)
You can also use this link
https://www.dspim.com/invest/#goto=kyc
to get your KYC modified online.
Attachments (1)
pdf
KYC Form - Individual_oct22 (1) (2) (1).pdf
352 KB

title: Why is Re-KYC required and for what type of KYC records?
content:
All

View all
Tickets

Sorry! nothing found for
Why is Re-KYC required and for what type of KYC records?
Modified on Wed, 13 Nov, 2024 at  7:56 PM
According to a recent regulation from SEBI, it is now mandatory to have your KYC registered with Aadhaar to conduct transactions in Mutual funds.
Kindly find the different types of KYC status:
1. KYC Validated :
The customer’s KYC is registered with Aadhar card and the contact details are validated.
2. KYC registered:
a. The customer’s KYC is registered with Non-OVD documents like bank statement, utility bill, rental agreement.
b. The customer's KYC is registered with Aadhaar but the contact details are not validated.
c. The customer's KYC is registered with Non Aadhar OVD documents like Driving License, Voter ID, and Passport
KYC will be active in the below scenarios and the transactions will be processed seamlessly
1. KYC is validated
2. KYC registered with DL, Voter ID or Passport, can transact in Mutual funds that are invested before 1st April 2024.
Modification required for the below-mentioned KYCs :
1. If the KYC status is on hold or rejected, the KYC needs to be modified with Aadhar.
2. If the KYC is registered with DL, Voter ID or Passport, It is advised to complete the KYC online by submitting your Aadhaar as OVD proof, and giving a valid email id and mobile so that you can invest with any mutual funds in future.
In case it is not an Aadhaar-based KYC, you will have to submit the KYC documents every time you approach a new Mutual Fund for investment.
It is advised to complete the KYC online by submitting your Aadhaar as OVD proof, giving valid email id and mobile so that you can invest with any mutual funds in future.
Aadhaar-based: In case it is not an Aadhaar based KYC, you will have to submit the KYC documents every time you approach a new Mutual Fund for investment.

title: What’s new at Scripbox?
meta_description: We have done a complete overhaul and one can expect smoother journeys, easier navigation, simpler interface, and more powerful ways to organise your wealth.
content:
All
Articles
Recent Searches
Clear all
No recent searches
Popular Articles
Articles
View all
Topics
View all
Tickets
View all
Sorry! nothing found for
What’s new at Scripbox?
Modified on Thu, 14 Nov, 2024 at 12:41 PM
Loan against mutual funds.
Here are a few new features that you can explore -
Loan is in the form of an Overdraft facility.
Interest is charged only on the amount utilised and for the period it is utilised.
Instant withdrawal/ payment of amount within the limit.
No Foreclosure/ Pre-payment charges.
Ideal choice for meeting unplanned short-term expenses like medical emergency, house renovation, holiday etc.
You should download the Mobile App using this link.
You will need to provide all device permissions while installing the Mobile App.
Sign up with an email & mobile number registered with RTAs.
Investments should be in Single name or joint with “Either/ or” mode of holding.
Tax Saver Funds, Close Ended Funds cannot be lien marked.
Provide bank account number enabled with net banking facility.
Unified Portfolio
Unified Portfolio + Financial Plan
Instead of having a separate plan & portfolio for each goal, you’ll have one unified portfolio and a single financial plan with multiple goals.
This change is driven by our clients’ growing wealth and increasing financial knowledge. The unified approach is more effective for managing your wealth than the previous goal-based portfolios.
Why is this better?
Simplified Risk Management: A single portfolio is easier to align with your risk profile.
Streamlined Investments: One SIP for all your goals
Fewer Fund Accounts: You’ll have fewer fund accounts to manage.
Holistic Planning: Easier to consider your family’s wealth outside of our platform when planning your financial goals.
Efficient Withdrawals: Once a goal is achieved, you can withdraw more efficiently from your total wealth, rather than from individual buckets.
Effective Portfolio Management: Activities like portfolio reviews, asset allocation, and rebalancing can be done more effectively.
Greater Flexibility: You have more flexibility in changing, adding, or removing goals.
Direct Plan Subscription
Benefits for Regular & Direct (Individual) Plans
#1: Unbiased fund recommendations
We use a rule based algorithm to rank the entire universe of mutual funds and then recommend 22 funds in 14 curated categories - the ones we believe are the most relevant for individual investors. The rules ensure that any biases are eliminated.
We then publish the performance of our recommendations every year - good, bad or ugly. Our latest track record for FY'24 is publicly available
here
.
#2: Personalised portfolio construction
Picking the best funds in a category is not enough. Nor is investing in products recommended at random.
You need to invest in a manner appropriate to your goals, your financial position (including current investments) and your risk profile. We construct a portfolio which takes all of these into account, so that you can grow your wealth in a way that works for you.
#3: Continuous fund monitoring & exit alerts
The world is changing constantly and so does fund performance. We’ve been around long enough to know that a long term investing strategy has to be accompanied by continuous monitoring of a portfolio. Our algorithms do that for you and trigger alerts when you need to take action and exit a fund. We will also recommend where to re-invest that money.
#4: Comprehensive financial planning & goals
The primary reason we invest is to take care of our families’ future needs. Our financial planning tool is easily the best in the business giving you an actionable view of your financial future.
#5: Smart Withdraw™ algorithm to save tax
Rules for taxation of gains made on mutual fund investments are complex. Most of them work to your advantage though, if you know how, and take them into consideration before you make withdrawals from mutual funds. Smart Withdraw™ algorithm does this for you automatically.
#6: Convenient investing in 2000+ mutual funds
We partner with all the mutual fund companies - giving you the ability to invest in all of the 2000+ mutual funds. We are the only app where you have a choice of investing in both regular or direct funds.
#7: Detailed fund fact sheets with Scripbox opinion
Our research team reviews every fund category and mutual fund - creating an internal score which translates into investment worthiness for each fund. This is summarised in our easy to understand fact sheets. These fact sheets are superior to star ratings because they express our research view and not just the factual data.
#8: Risk profiling
Our two dimensional risk profiling algorithm uses both your ability to take risk (a matter of financial position) and your willingness to take risk (a psychological measure) to ensure that we recommend the most appropriate risk return combination to you.
#9: Right mix of equity/ debt/ gold/ US equity
Our portfolio construction combines these 4 asset classes—proven to have low correlations—to create a diversified portfolio for you. The asset allocation is a function of your objective, time to objective (Glide path) and your risk profile and serves to balance risk and return for you.
#10: Right number of funds
How many funds in your portfolio depends on the size of your portfolio and risk management. Too many funds create clutter and diffuse the impact of fund selection, while too few funds may lead to concentration risk. Our algorithms balance this with minimum investing thresholds imposed by fund houses to recommend the right number of funds to you.
#11: Algorithmic prompts for better investing
Investing is a long term process and we ease your way through this with prompts at different times to ensure that you take the actions you need to, when you need to.
#12: Capital gains reports for tax filing
When it’s time to file taxes, you get a consolidated capital gains report across all mutual funds to help make the process less painful for you.
#13: Track all investments, insurance & loans
Knowing where your money is makes it easy to plan for your future. With Scripbox, you can track all your mutual funds, stocks, FDs, EPFO, PPF, Insurance and loans. Our financial planning tool automatically picks these up and considers them for meeting your future goals and to identify gaps, if any.
#14: Transfer fund portfolios to Scripbox
You can also transfer all of your regular funds, wherever invested, to Scripbox so that you not only get a 360 degree view of your investments but also be able to use our advisory recommendations & transaction capability.
#15: Available when you need us: 8 am to 8 pm, 7 days a week customer support
Last but perhaps the most important, we have a customer support team that’s available to you for any queries or help. We work extended timings: all seven days of the week and from 8 am to 8 pm.
You can know more about the direct plan
here
.

Portfolio Health Logic Updates
Modified on Thu, 14 Nov, 2024 at 12:49 PM
What are the exit conditions?
1. Exits only from non-recommended funds
2. Small holding exits (<2% of total portfolio)
3. No exits for recommended funds or top-ranked funds from recommended categories
What’s in place?
1. Recommend the best funds from the Scripbox recommendation
2. Primarily focusing on portfolio clutter
3. Notifications for you to do your portfolio health check on the App/email notifications
FAQs
Q: Will the external portfolio have similar exit rules?
Yes, the external portfolio also will respect the same exit rules.
Q: Can you continue to modify exit recommendations?
Yes, this will continue to remain available.

Log in to your Scripbox account
Click on Profile tab > Personal Information under profile settings
Click on Name
Enter your name
Click on Update

2. Once the new name is updated on the PAN card, the name in the bank records should be updated
.
3. Update the correct name on the Aadhar card(as KYC validation is required) Additionally, you are also requirem to share a copy of your PAN card with the updated name for verification purposes. Once verified, we will provide the list of documents needed for the name change process.
The name change process involves three steps
:
1. KYC Modification
2. Updating the new name in the existing folios
3. Updating the new name in the Scripbox records
For updating the new name in KYC (7-10 working days) (Scanned and Physical Copies):
Self-signed PAN
Self-signed Aadhaar card
KYC form: Kindly affix your passport-size photo, sign across the same and also sign on the first and second page in the given slot (Applicant E sign and Applicant Wet signature). Please do not fill in any other details, we will do what is needed according to the documents you will share with us. Attached is the KYC form).
If this is a post-marriage name change, you must share the Marriage Certificate with the Notarisation.
Name Change Affidavit (varies by fund houses):
a. For most fund houses, a notarised affidavit on Rs.100/- stamp paper or a Gazette notification is required.
b.For Franklin Templeton, a notarised affidavit on Rs.200/- stamp paper is mandatory.
Note: The name change process is offline and we would need the physical documents to be attested by a scheduled Indian bank in India or from a Notary Public or by any Gazette Government Official and then couriered to our office address.
We request you share the coloured scanned copies of the above documents for the name change via email for pre-verification. Post confirmation that documents are good to proceed, they are required to be couriered to our office address.
Office address:
Scripbox Wealth Managers Pvt Ltd, Wilshire III by MFAR, 5th Floor, 492, Hobli, RHB Colony, Mahadevapura, Bengaluru, Karnataka- 560048
For updating the new name in existing folios(7-10 working days)
(Scanned and Physical Copies)
:
New bank proof (either a cancelled cheque leaf/ bank statement) with the new name mentioned on it. If you are submitting a bank statement with your new name, it should be the latest bank statement with the recent three months transactions sheet along with the banker's attestion of Bank Manager or above (round seal, signature, designation, name, and employee code)
Name Change Request Letter (Manual form - which we share with the customer once the PAN with the new name is shared. The customer has to download the form, sign it (wet signature), and send it to us.
For updating the new name in your Scripbox account (1 working day):
Once the new name is updated in the KYC as well as at the folios, the new name will be updated in the Scripbox records.
If we need any additional documents, we shall let you know accordingly. The change of name change usually takes 4 weeks from the time we have received the complete set of documents.
Attachments (1)
pdf
KYC Form - Individual.pdf
352 KB

I want to change my contact details registered with Scripbox, how do I do this?
Modified on Thu, 14 Nov, 2024 at  1:05 PM
You can update your email with Scripbox by following the below steps:
1. Log in to your Scripbox account
2. Click on
Profile
tab (1) >
Personal Information
(2) under profile settings
3. Click on Email (if your current email is not verified, you will see "Verify" here, and will have to verify your email.
4. Enter your new email
5. On clicking “Update”, you will be prompted to confirm if an email change linked to your investments is required
6. If not, your email with Scripbox will get updated after verifying the OTP sent to your updated email.
Note: This change in Email will only reflect for Scripbox communications and will not change your email in your investment account (i.e. the email linked to your investments) This process is complete, all emails/SMS will go to your new email ID/contact number.

Sorry! nothing found for
I want to update the email linked to my investments, how can I do this?
Modified on Thu, 14 Nov, 2024 at  1:37 PM
The email change/update process involves three steps:
1. Creating the request for email change from your Scripbox account
2. Updating the new email in the existing folios
3. Updating the new email  in the Scripbox records
Steps to raise the email change request via the app:
a. Login into your Scripbox account
b. Click on Profile (towards the right-hand side corner on the top of the home page)
c. Click on the investor name - Under Investments - Click on Scripbox - Click on Change under Email id
d. Edit the email id and Click on Update Changes
Post which it will direct you to the
MF central page
, where you can update the email id via online. The details will be updated in the folios within 1 working day.
If the email id change request fails, then you are required to undergo an offline process.
For updating the new contact number at the folios (10-13 working days) (Scanned Copies):
a. Self-signed email id change request (same will be sent by Scripbox
b. Self-signed PAN card copy.
Once we receive the documents, the new email id will updated across all the folios.
For updating the new email id in your Scripbox account (1 working day):
Once the new email id is updated in all the folios, the new email id will be updated in the Scripbox records.
If we need any additional documents, we shall let you know accordingly. The change of email id usually takes 2 weeks from the time we have received the complete set of documents.

You can set password only if you have both verified (i.e. OTP authentication done) email ID and mobile number. You can set password by following the below procedures:
1. Log in to your Scripbox account.
2. Click on Profile tab > Personal Information under profile settings.
3. Click on “Set Password”.
4. Enter & re-enter your Password.
5. Click on Set Password.
Once done your password will get set. Note: if the current password is entered incorrectly more than 5 times, the user will be locked and logged out of the Scripbox app. The user will automatically get unlocked after 24 hrs or will have to reset password to get unlocked immediately.

title: What is the right plan to invest in?
meta_description: When looking for the right Scripbox plan to invest in, it's important to think about your financial goals, your risk appetite, and how long you want to invest

What is the right plan to invest in?
Modified on Thu, 14 Nov, 2024 at  3:32 PM
When looking for the right Scripbox plan to invest in, it's important to think about your financial goals, your risk appetite, and how long you want to invest. Here are some steps you can follow -
Set your financial goals:
Clearly define what you want to achieve financially, like saving for retirement, buying a house, or paying for your child's education.
Assess your risk tolerance:
Consider how much risk you're comfortable with. This will help you choose a growth strategy that matches your risk preferences.
Think about your investment timeline:
Decide how long you're willing to invest your money. Short-term goals may call for more conservative investments, while long-term goals might allow for more aggressive options.
By taking these factors into account and exploring the available Scripbox plans, you can make an informed decision and select the plan that's the best fit for you.

Yes, you can have multiple investors contributing to an existing plan.
At Scripbox, we design to be family first. Sharing plans across family members allows us to provide better advisory recommendations and give you better control over asset allocation to achieve the goal on time. We believe that involving multiple investors in a plan can enhance the overall effectiveness and alignment with your family's financial goals.

title: What is the difference in the Unified portfolio and how is it better than previous goal based option?
content:
All
Articles
Recent Searches
Clear all
No recent searches
Popular Articles
Articles
View all
Topics
View all
Tickets
View all
Sorry! nothing found for
What is the difference in the Unified portfolio and how is it better than previous goal based option?
Modified on Thu, 14 Nov, 2024 at  3:43 PM
Instead of having a separate plan & portfolio for each goal, you’ll have one unified portfolio and a single financial plan with multiple goals.
Financial Plan gives a flexible way to manage goals - including open-ended goals like Emergency.
1. Simplified Risk Management: A single portfolio is easier to align with your risk profile.
2. Streamlined Investments: One SIP for all your goals
3. Fewer Fund Accounts: You’ll have fewer fund accounts to manage.
4. Holistic Planning: Easier to consider your family’s wealth outside of our platform when planning your financial goals.
5. Efficient Withdrawals: Once a goal is achieved, you can withdraw more efficiently from your total wealth, rather than from individual buckets.
6. Effective Portfolio Management: Activities like portfolio reviews, asset allocation, and rebalancing can be done more effectively.
7. Greater Flexibility: You have more flexibility in changing, adding, or removing goals.

Once we understand your risk profile, we can determine the appropriate growth strategy for your plan. Our research team has developed four evidence-based strategies based on market behaviour across different asset classes since 1997. These strategies are:
Active Growth
Balanced Growth
Safe Growth
Minimum Risk
You can choose any of these strategies to align with your investment preferences. Additionally, if you prefer more control over your portfolio, you can create a custom strategy as well.
It's important to note that our SIP or One-time investment recommendations and Portfolio Health rebalance recommendations will be in line with your chosen growth strategy. This ensures that your investment decisions and portfolio management are in line with your desired approach.

title: Why should I understand my Risk profile?
meta_description: Understanding your risk profile is important because it helps you align your investments with your comfort level and financial goals.
content:

Sorry! nothing found for
Why should I understand my Risk profile?
Modified on Fri, 30 Jun, 2023 at  3:14 PM
Understanding your risk profile is important because it helps you align your investments with your comfort level and financial goals. By knowing your risk tolerance, you can choose investments that match your preferences for potential returns and fluctuations. It also helps you stay resilient during market fluctuations and make informed decisions that support your long-term investment strategy.
Your risk profile reflects two important aspects of your risk appetite -
Ability to take risks
Willingness to take risks
By comprehending your comfort level, we can provide personalised recommendations for an optimal growth strategy.
Note that our recommendations for Systematic Investment Plans (SIP) and Portfolio Health will consistently align with your chosen growth strategy, ensuring that your investment objectives are respected.

title: Do you actively rebalance the portfolio based on growth strategy?
meta_description: Active rebalancing involves exiting and reinvesting based on specific objectives, such as recommendations from the Portfolio Health check or fund quality.
content:
All
Articles

Clear all

View all
Tickets
View all
Sorry! nothing found for
Do you actively rebalance the portfolio based on growth strategy?
Modified on Fri, 30 Jun, 2023 at  3:15 PM
Active rebalancing involves exiting and reinvesting based on specific objectives, such as recommendations from the Portfolio Health check or fund quality. However, it's important to note that active rebalancing may incur exit load and taxes. The benefits of active rebalancing should outweigh the impact of these charges.
On the other hand, passive rebalancing focuses on adjusting the structure of your ongoing SIPs and one-time investments to align with your objective. The growth strategy employs passive rebalancing, where our fund recommendations constantly strive to bring your portfolio closer to the target allocation set by your growth strategy.

title: How does Scripbox pick funds when withdrawing?
meta_description: Scripbox Smart WithdrawTM algorithm is designed to optimise your tax outgo by considering a combination of long-term and short-term units
content:
All

Sorry! nothing found for
How does Scripbox pick funds when withdrawing?
Modified on Fri, 30 Jun, 2023 at  3:20 PM
Scripbox Smart Withdraw
TM
algorithm is designed to optimise your tax outgo by considering a combination of long-term and short-term units. It analyses multiple scenarios to arrive at the right fund mix that minimises taxes.
The algorithm takes advantage of tax offsetting provisions provided by the Income Tax Department. This allows you to adjust gains in certain funds against losses in others, optimising your overall tax liability.

title: Why is the money credited to my bank account different from the amount I placed the withdrawal for?
meta_description: If you try to withdraw only long-term units or try to withdraw completely from a fund, the final money credited may be different from what you saw when you placed the withdrawal.
content:
All
Articles
Recent Searches
Clear all
No recent searches
Popular Articles
Articles
View all
Topics
View all
Tickets
View all
Sorry! nothing found for
Why is the money credited to my bank account different from the amount I placed the withdrawal for?
Modified on Fri, 30 Jun, 2023 at  3:22 PM
If you try to withdraw only long-term units or try to withdraw completely from a fund (or set of funds), the final money credited may be different from what you saw when you placed the withdrawal.
This happens because both these modes are unit-based withdrawals. You’re essentially redeeming units for these funds from the mutual fund company, and the value of each unit,called NAV,changes daily.
The NAV that gets honoured is the NAV on the day the withdrawal is processed by the mutual fund company. This can be 2-5 business days from the date of placing the withdrawal order.

title: What is the withdrawal cut-off time?
content:
All
Articles

Popular Articles
Articles
View all
Topics
View all
Tickets
View all
Sorry! nothing found for
What is the withdrawal cut-off time?
Modified on Fri, 27 Jun, 2025 at 12:01 PM
Withdrawal requests are processed in two batches:
Withdrawal requests placed before 12:00 PM are processed on the same working day in the first batch, while requests placed between 12:00 PM and 1:30 PM are processed in the second batch on the same working day.
This cut-off applies to Equity, Debt, Liquid, and ELSS funds (excluding Overnight funds).
Requests placed after 1:30 PM will be processed on the next working day.
For Overnight funds, withdrawal requests can be placed until 6:00 PM for the instructions to be processed on the same working day.

title: When are the mutual fund units allotted to me?
content:
All
Articles
Recent Searches
Clear all
No recent searches
Popular Articles
Articles
View all
Topics
View all
Tickets
View all
Sorry! nothing found for
When are the mutual fund units allotted to me?
Modified on Fri, 27 Jun, 2025 at 12:04 PM
The date of allotment of mutual fund units depends on the payment method selected by you to make the investment.
Investment via UPI:
If you transfer money using UPI before 12:30 PM on a working day, units will be allotted on the same day.
If transferred after 12:30 PM, units will be allotted on the next working day.
Scripbox will show you the exact investment date.
Investment via Net Banking:
If you transfer money using Net Banking anytime between 12:00 AM and 11:59 PM on a working day, units will be allotted on the next working day (T+1) — regardless of the time you place the instruction.
Automated transfer (linked bank account):
Units will be allotted within the next working day from the scheduled investment date, and the Scripbox dashboard will be updated within 2 working days.
In the event of any exceptional delay in unit allocation due to unforeseen circumstances, we will notify you via email. Any difference in the Net Asset Value (NAV) arising from such a delay will be refunded
*
to your bank account registered with Scripbox.
*Please note that any NAV difference of more than ₹50 will be refunded.

How much time will it take for a transaction to be processed?
Modified on Fri, 30 Jun, 2023 at  3:19 PM
For Netbanking transactions, your transaction will be completed typically within 2 business days.
For UPI transactions, the completion time can range between 1-2 business days, depending on the timing of your investment relative to the
cutoff time

For SIP transactions, the completion time can vary between 3-5 business days from the moment the money is debited from your bank account. The actual duration depends on the type of bank mandate you have in place.

meta_description: A bank mandate is an authorisation you provide to your bank, allowing Scripbox to transfer funds to the mutual fund company as per your scheduled investment.
content:

Sorry! nothing found for
What is a bank mandate? Why do you need to register one?
Modified on Fri, 30 Jun, 2023 at  3:24 PM
A bank mandate is an authorisation you provide to your bank, allowing Scripbox to transfer funds to the mutual fund company as per your scheduled investment transactions. It enables the automation of your investments and is highly recommended for all SIP investments.
Please note that if you choose not to register a bank mandate, you can still invest via Net banking or UPI. You will receive a reminder email three working days before the investment date, providing you with the option to invest through Net banking or UPI.

title: How to register a bank mandate via net banking?
meta_description: During the investment journey, when you reach the payment options screen for a SIP, you will be prompted to register for a bank mandate

Sorry! nothing found for
How to register a bank mandate via net banking?
Modified on Thu, 14 Nov, 2024 at  4:20 PM
During the investment journey, when you reach the payment options screen for a SIP, you will be prompted to register for a bank mandate. By clicking on it, you will be redirected to our payment partner's mandate registration portal. There, you can review and verify the details of the mandate you will be authorising.
To authorise the mandate, you will be directed to your registered bank's net banking portal. Once you have completed the verification process, your bank mandate will be immediately activated.
You can also register the bank mandate online by following the below steps
* Login to Scripbox App
* Click on manage
* Select the SIP
* Click on modify investment and continue
*Click on proceed to payment
* Select the bank mandate option and click on register bank mandate
* Complete the bank mandate using your Netbanking user ID and password

title: What is Portfolio Health Review?
meta_description: The Portfolio Health review is a periodic intervention mechanism that evaluates each individual mutual fund in the portfolio.
content:

Sorry! nothing found for
What is Portfolio Health Review?
Modified on Tue, 27 Jun, 2023 at  6:35 PM
Scripbox Portfolio Health will supercharge your family’s investments by helping you determine which funds to exit and which funds to reinvest in. It is a quarterly automated assessment of your portfolio. During this review, Scripbox evaluates all the funds in your portfolio based on 9 critical parameters and assigns a score, ranging from a low of 1 to a high of 100, to each of them. This helps you -
Personalise fund recommendations
Declutter portfolio
Realign investments to the right funds for you

Modified on Tue, 27 Jun, 2023 at  6:32 PM
With Scripbox Portfolio Health, we evaluate your family investment portfolio, including both Scripbox investments and external investments. By considering your entire portfolio, we provide you with more accurate and personalised recommendations. This comprehensive assessment helps you gain a clear understanding of the strengths and weaknesses of your investments, empowering you to make well-informed decisions. The best part is that Portfolio Health automatically conducts this review every quarter, ensuring that you stay on track towards achieving your financial goals without any hassle or worry.

title: What will happen if I don't take actions on my portfolio recommendations?
meta_description: You might potentially have not-so-good performing mutual funds in your portfolio that are at risk and might hamper your growth or might even potentially erode your capital.
content:

Sorry! nothing found for
What will happen if I don't take actions on my portfolio recommendations?
Modified on Tue, 27 Jun, 2023 at  6:33 PM
If you don’t act on your recommendations, you may potentially have underperforming or at-risk funds in your portfolio. This may hamper portfolio growth and potentially deplete your capital. Some of the risks that Portfolio Health analyses your portfolio on include concentration risk, credit risk, interest rate risk, market risk, and more. Hence, it is advisable to consistently review your portfolio every quarter and act on your recommendations.

title: Portfolio review is available on other platforms? Why should I trust Scripbox's recommendations?
meta_description: Scripbox don't just review your mutual fund investments with Scripbox but also look at your external investments.
content:

Sorry! nothing found for
Portfolio review is available on other platforms? Why should I trust Scripbox's recommendations?
Modified on Thu, 29 Jun, 2023 at  5:53 PM
We don't just review your mutual fund investments with Scripbox but also look at your external investments.
We allow you to seamlessly sync and update all your investments outside the Scripbox platform.
We consider all your family investments and give recommendations at a consolidated level which is the right way.
We consider the funds present under each plan and based on the asset allocation of these plans, we suggest reinvestment recommendations thereby honouring each plan's growth strategy.
This way, reinvestment recommendations are given to help you achieve your financial goals with your desired risk appetite.

How often will Scripbox suggest reviewing my mutual fund portfolio health?
Modified on Thu, 14 Nov, 2024 at  5:11 PM
We recommend reviewing your entire portfolio on a quarterly basis. This regular monitoring helps maintain stability in your portfolio and prevents large exits. It's important to note that reviewing it more frequently than once every quarter can increase anxiety without necessarily improving outcomes. By adhering to a quarterly review schedule, you can avoid unnecessary churn in your investments.

Sorry! nothing found for
I got an error message saying that my account is suspended. What does this mean?
Modified on Tue, 27 Jun, 2023 at  3:20 PM
If there is an ongoing bank change request in your account, we temporarily suspend your account. This precautionary measure ensures that debits are not initiated in the wrong bank account, preventing unnecessary charges. Once the bank change request is successfully processed and completed, you will be able to resume your Portfolio Health actions.

title: How can I import my existing Mutual Funds investment with Scripbox?
meta_description: You can easily track your mutual funds by generating a CAMS Mutual Funds CAS. Once you receive an email from CAMS with your CAS

Sorry! nothing found for
How can I import my existing Mutual Funds investment with Scripbox?
Modified on Thu, 14 Nov, 2024 at  5:31 PM
Scripbox has integrated with MFCentral to bring you your Mutual Funds instantly, simply by providing your PAN number and the mobile number associated with your investments.
Steps to import Mutual Funds held outside Scripbox:
Open the Wealth tab in the Scripbox app
Scroll to the bottom of the screen
Under  track  option
Click on add your external investments
Choose which investor's mutual funds to import and click on continue
Select Add External Investment
Choose Mutual Funds and click on Continue
Enter your phone number or email ID, along with your PAN
Enter the OTP generated by MFCentral
That's it! It might take up to 5 minutes to complete importing your full Mutual Fund holdings list if you have a lot of investments.
Please note: If you try to import the Mutual Funds of any PAN that is not present in your account, you will be asked to tag it to an existing family member or add a new family member with that PAN. This is so that your Wealth calculations remain accurate.

I have a Fixed Deposit that has matured, but it’s still showing as part of my Total Wealth. Why?
Modified on Fri, 30 Jun, 2023 at  6:30 PM
For Fixed Deposits which are manually tracked on the Scripbox app, we cannot determine what happens to the money when it matures. You could reinvest it, leave it in a savings account, or use it for other purposes. As a result, you have full control over your wealth and can choose to delete the amount if you no longer want to track it within the app.

Sorry! nothing found for
How can I move my investments  from another platform to Scripbox?
Modified on Sat, 9 Aug, 2025 at 10:20 AM
To transfer investments from another platform to Scripbox, You need to generate the consolidated statements and email us to
help@scripbox.com
or you can Whatsapp it to us on +91-8884448026 mentioning which all funds you need to do the broker chnage so that we can prepare the broker change form manually and share with you. Once you receive the form from us .
Kindly take a print-out of the forms and share the signed documents along with the self-attested pan card copy (all clear scanned copies) email us to
help@scripbox.com
or you can Whatsapp it to us on +91-8884448026
The time frame for the broker code change process to be completed is 15 working days. We will keep you updated on the progress of the broker change.
Please find the below steps how to generate ECAS.
Click on the CAMS website -
Get Consolidated Account Statement - CAMS+KFintech
Click on Statement Type.
Click on Detailed.
Click on Specific Period.
Choose date from 01/01/1990 (It is default date).
Folio Listing : With Zero Balanced Folios.Registered Email Address (You can not copy paste).
Please enter the respective password. (If it is not working or if there is any error after submit let us know right away)Success Message from CAMS website.

title: How do I delete investments that I have tracked from my account?
meta_description:
Open the Profile tab
Choose the investor whose investments you want to delete
Find the product you want to delete and click on it
content:
All
Articles
Recent Searches
Clear all

Articles
View all
Topics
View all
Tickets
View all
Sorry! nothing found for
How do I delete investments that I have tracked from my account?
Modified on Thu, 14 Nov, 2024 at  5:57 PM
There are 2 ways you can delete your investments tracked on Scripbox:
From the Profile tab
Log in via the App
Click on the Profile Tab
Under Investors<Select your account
Click on External MF Account
Click on Delete Account, and confirm Yes.
Directly from the individual investment/loan/insurance policy:
Open the Wealth Dashboard
Click on "Products" and select the product type (i.e. specific investment/loan/insurance) that you want to delete
You will be taken to a list of that particular product type. Find the product you want to delete and click on it.
On this page, below the details of that specific folio or product, you will see a "Delete" option.

title: Unable to download the app on Android
content:

Sorry! nothing found for
Unable to download the app on Android
Modified on Thu, 10 Aug, 2023 at 10:31 AM
I would recommend to clear the cache and data for the Play Store app and the device’s download manager. This will clear any settings and temporary information saved to the device. You can still access purchases you’ve made from Google Play, like apps, music, movies, and books.
Open your device's main
Settings
app.
Tap
Apps
or
Application manager
(depending on your device, this may be different).
Tap
Google Play Store
.
Tap
Storage
.
Tap
CLEAR CACHE
>
OK
.
Tap
CLEAR DATA
>
OK
.
Tap the back arrow to go back to the main menu.
Select
Downloads
or
Download Manager
.
Tap
CLEAR CACHE
>
OK
.
Tap
CLEAR DATA
>
OK
.
Relaunch the app.
You can also use Google's
help center
for troubleshooting issues with apps. Some troubleshooting steps may reset your Play Store app settings. So if you previously turned on
purchase protection
or
parental controls
, make sure to check the settings when you’re done.

What if I fail to pay your fee?
Modified on Wed, 24 Apr, 2024 at  4:23 PM
If you fail to pay your fee for 15 days your account will be temporarily suspended. Once the fee is paid, your account will be reactivated again
If your account remains suspended for more than 3 months, it will be closed and you will need to register again.
Your mutual fund holdings will remain unaffected and we will help you transfer them to another provider upon request.

Sorry! nothing found for
How do I add an investor, my child, to my Scripbox investment account?
Modified on Mon, 21 Oct, 2024 at  8:55 AM
You can easily create a Scripbox account for any family member by clicking on "Add an Investor" within your Scripbox account. When adding a child, please note the following:
Bank Account Details: Provide the bank account details that are in your child's name.
Same Guardian: The guardian of the minor's bank account and the guardian of the minor's Scripbox account must be the same person.
Joint Bank Account: If you are adding the details of a joint bank account, ensure that your child is one of the bank account holders, preferably the primary account holder.

Sorry! nothing found for
How do I set my password?
Modified on Mon, 21 Oct, 2024 at  9:18 AM
You can set password only if you have both verified (i.e. OTP authentication done) email ID and mobile number. You can set password by following the below procedures:
1. Log in to your Scripbox account.
2. Click on Profile tab > Personal Information under profile settings.
3. Click on “Set Password”.
4. Enter & re-enter your Password.
5. Click on Set Password.
Once done your password will get set. Note: if the current password is entered incorrectly more than 5 times, the user will be locked and logged out of the Scripbox app. The user will automatically get unlocked after 24 hrs or will have to reset password to get unlocked immediately.

content:

Sorry! nothing found for
How can I delete my account with Scripbox?
Modified on Mon, 21 Oct, 2024 at  9:22 AM
You can delete your Scripbox account by emailing us at help@scripbox.com. You can also call us at 1800-102-1265 or whatsapp us on +91-8884448026.
Upon receiving your request from the registered email ID with Scripbox, we will delete your account and all its data within 1 working day and send you a confirmation. One of us may also reach out to you by phone or e-mail to understand your reasons for closure better.
Please note that in case any investment instruction (including an incomplete or failed one) is present in your account, we will not be able to delete your account. This is in line with processes followed by all financial firms. But, we will close the account and ensure that you don’t receive any emails or phone calls from our end.

Sorry! nothing found for
How to register a bank mandate via net banking?
Modified on Mon, 21 Oct, 2024 at  9:27 AM
During the investment journey, when you reach the payment options screen for a SIP, you will be prompted to register for a bank mandate. By clicking on it, you will be redirected to our payment partner's mandate registration portal. There, you can review and verify the details of the mandate you will be authorising.
To authorise the mandate, you will be directed to your registered bank's net banking portal. Once you have completed the verification process, your bank mandate will be immediately activated.

Sorry! nothing found for
What are staggered investments? Why are certain reinvestment transactions of mine displayed as staggered?
Modified on Tue, 27 Jun, 2023 at  2:48 PM
Staggered investments provide a solution to prevent order cancellations and the creation of multiple investment folios due to bank mandate limitations. By splitting the reinvestment amount into multiple transactions spread over a few days, you can ensure smooth exit and reinvest transactions. To learn more about staggering investments, you can find additional information
here
.
If you prefer not to stagger your investments, the most effective approach to avoiding order cancellations is to increase your bank mandate limit. Please contact us for assistance.

My child has recently become a major. Could you please advise if any changes are needed for his investments??
Modified on Fri, 6 Mar at 10:02 AM
Things to be done by the customer before contacting us for the conversion :
1. Apply for a PAN card, once the Minor turns Major
2. Upgrade for a Savings bank account in the bank as the Minor turns into a Major
The Minor to Major conversion process involves three steps:
1. KYC registration of Major/account creation with Scripbox
2. Updation of new PAN and bank account details in the existing folios.
3. Transfer of folios from the Minor’s account to the Major’s account with Scripbox
KYC registration of Major/account creation with Scripbox
The Major’s KYC needs to be registered and validated. You are required to create a new Scripbox account by adding the major as a family member in your Scripbox account and the complete the sign up.
Steps to add yourself as a family member
Once the sign up is completed, it takes 2 hours for the account to be activated.
For updating the new details in the minor’s investments (12-15 working days) (Scanned and Physical Copies):
A copy of your child’s PAN with their signature on it.
New Bank Proof - A cancelled cheque leaf with the account holder's name printed on it OR a bank statement
with the recent three months transaction sheet along with banker's attestation by Bank Manager or above (round seal, signature, designation, name, and employee code)
The request letter to AMC is to update your child’s old folios having minor status with major status. The letter must be signed by the guardian and the new account holder. The letter is attached. (FATCA and CRS information, Nomination details should be filled by account holder(name),
Old Bank Proof - A cancelled cheque with the name printed and the guardian details or a bank statement mentioning the account holder's name and guardian details and transaction details, along
with the recent three months transaction sheet along with banker's attestation by Bank Manager or above (round seal, signature, designation, name, and employee code)
Signature Verification Letter (attached in the mail) We request you print this form with the banker's letterhead format and attested by the banker with the employee name, employee ID, & designation
Cams & Karvy FATCA form (Attached) (To be filled and signed by a Major)
Signed Nominee updation form (Attached) (Do not fill in any folio details in the form)
In the M2M form, the details are not required to be filled by the client. We will complete the required information based on the documents submitted.
We request you share the coloured scanned copies of the above documents via email for pre-verification. Post confirmation that documents are good to proceed, they are required to be couriered to our office address.
Office address:
Scripbox Wealth Managers Pvt Ltd, Wilshire III by MFAR, 5th Floor, 492, Hobli, RHB Colony, Mahadevapura, Bengaluru, Karnataka- 560048
Movement of folios (1 working day):
Once the major’s account is active, we will move the folios from the major’s account to the minor’s account.
If we need any additional documents, we shall let you know accordingly. The change of minor to major usually takes 4 weeks from the time we have received the complete set of documents.

title: Why do you suggest graded exit on Neutral fund?
content:
All
Articles
Recent Searches
Clear all
No recent searches
Popular Articles
Articles
View all
Topics
View all
Tickets
View all
Sorry! nothing found for
Why do you suggest graded exit on Neutral fund?
Modified on Thu, 14 Nov, 2024 at  5:16 PM
Neutral funds score varies between 35 to 72.
We believe trimming the portfolio on the neutral fund is a better approach than just holding on to them without acting on it.
The Same treatment cannot be given to a fund of score 36 viz-a-viz fund with a score of 70. Hence, we suggest trimming ( withdrawing in a graded manner ) with a varied percentage based on the score.
Lesser the score, the higher the trimming, and the higher the score lesser the trim.
This will ensure that tomorrow if the neutral fund with a score of 70 goes above 72, we would not have asked you to exit much. Also, if a fund had a score of 36 changed in a few months to a score of 32, then we would have trimmed the investment in this fund to a larger extent already and you wouldn't have had left with many units in this fund. We also have considered your tax impact and considered graded exit versus full exit at once. The Graded exit of neutral funds over a period of time, might help reduce the exit load/ tax impact as well.

You recommended withdrawal from a fund, but I still see some units of the same fund in my portfolio. Can you explain what is full exit / partial exits?
Modified on Thu, 14 Nov, 2024 at  5:22 PM
If the fund that you have invested in is not a recommended fund, meaning the score is less than 35, then we suggest a full exit from this fund.
But if we have a neutral view on a fund where the score is between 35 to 72 and not a part of Scripbox recommended fund, then we perform a graded exit on these funds.
That is why you see partial exit recommendations and this marked for graded exit.

title: How does the Portfolio Health review work?
content:
All
Articles
Recent Searches
Clear all
No recent searches
Popular Articles
Articles
View all
Topics
View all
Tickets
View all
Sorry! nothing found for
How does the Portfolio Health review work?
Modified on Thu, 14 Nov, 2024 at  5:13 PM
Portfolio Health evaluates each fund in your portfolio by assigning a score ranging from 1 to 100. This score is based on a comprehensive assessment of 22 variables across performance, consistency, risk, and more. These variables are categorised into 9 parameters.Funds with a score below 35 are considered unhealthy and are recommended for immediate full exit. Funds with a score between 35 and 72 are deemed neutral, indicating they have the potential to swing in either direction. These neutral funds are trimmed over a period of time with graded exit, allowing for continuous monitoring. During the next review cycle, if a neutral fund's score improves to a desirable level, it won't be recommended for exit. However, if the score decreases, it will be flagged for exit again. This process continues until your portfolio achieves complete health over 4 to 5 review cycles.

I want to update/change/add the nominee details registered to my investments, how can I do this?
Modified on Sat, 20 Dec, 2025 at 12:26 PM
The nominee change  process involves three steps:
1. Creating the request for nominee change/updation from your Scripbox account
2. Updating the new nominee details in the existing folios
3. Updating the new nominee details  in the Scripbox records
Steps to raise the nominee change request via the app:
a. Login into your Scripbox account
b. Click on Profile (towards the right-hand side corner on the top of the home page)
c. Click on the investor name - Under Investments - Click on Scripbox - Click on Change under Nominee
d. Edit the nominee details and Click on Update Changes
Post which you will be redirected to the MF central page, where you can update the nominee details online. The details will be updated in the folios within 1 working day.
If the nominee change request fails, then you are required to undergo an offline process as mentioned below.
For updating the new nominee details at the folios (10-13 working days) (Scanned Copies):
a. Self-signed nominee change request (same will be sent by Scripbox)
b. Self-signed PAN card copy.
Once we receive the documents, the new nominee details will updated across all the folios.
For updating the new nominee details in your Scripbox account (1 working day):
Once the new nominee is updated in all the folios, the new nominee will be updated in the Scripbox records.
If we need any additional documents, we shall let you know accordingly. The change of nominee details usually takes 2 weeks from the time we have received the complete set of documents.

Exit load refers to the fee imposed by mutual fund companies when investors withdraw their investments within a specific timeframe from the date of the initial investment. The duration of this period varies across different funds. It's important to note that Scripbox does not deduct this amount from your investments. Instead, the exit load is directly debited by the mutual fund companies themselves.

Sorry! nothing found for
Are my recommended reinvestments funds in alignment with my chosen asset allocation?
Modified on Thu, 14 Nov, 2024 at  5:19 PM
Certainly! The reinvestment recommendations we provide are all about matching your chosen asset allocation. We make sure to tailor the recommendations according to the specific plan you have in place.
For instance, if we suggest withdrawing ₹20,000 from two funds in your Long Term Portfolio, we'll direct the reinvestment back into that same portfolio. The number of reinvestment funds and the recommended sub-asset classes will depend on the asset allocation you've set for the Long Term Portfolio plan.
Let's say you initially wanted a 70% equity allocation in your Long Term Portfolio, but over time, your current equity exposure dropped to 65%. In that case, our reinvestment fund suggestions will lean towards more equity funds. We want to help you get closer to your desired 70% equity allocation and achieve the right asset mix for this plan.
Remember, this adjustment of your asset allocation can happen gradually as we provide you with suitable reinvestment options. Our goal is to ensure your investments align with your preferences and objectives.

Yes, you will be considered as a US/Canada based investor. There are limited set of fund houses that allow transactions for US / Canada investors. Our advisory recommendations are disabled because of the dearth of top ranked funds that we can work with to construct your portfolio.

How can I delete my account with Scripbox?
Modified on Wed, 28 Jun, 2023 at 11:33 AM
You can delete your Scripbox account by emailing us at help@scripbox.com. You can also call us at 1800-102-1265 or whatsapp us on +91-8884448026.
Upon receiving your request from the registered email ID with Scripbox, we will delete your account and all its data within 1 working day and send you a confirmation. One of us may also reach out to you by phone or e-mail to understand your reasons for closure better.
Please note that in case any investment instruction (including an incomplete or failed one) is present in your account, we will not be able to delete your account. This is in line with processes followed by all financial firms. But, we will close the account and ensure that you don’t receive any emails or phone calls from our end.

title: What is a Switch transaction? Why does it only occur during review of my external funds?
content:
All
Articles
Recent Searches
Clear all
No recent searches
Popular Articles
Articles
View all
Topics
View all
Tickets
View all
Sorry! nothing found for
What is a Switch transaction? Why does it only occur during review of my external funds?
Modified on Tue, 27 Jun, 2023 at  2:49 PM
When reviewing your investments outside of Scripbox, we have a convenient and hassle-free process for handling fund exits. To simplify the procedure, we temporarily switch from an equity fund to a liquid fund of the same mutual fund company. This enables us to track the transaction and provide you with regular updates on its status. Moreover, it reduces paperwork for you. Once the amount is successfully transferred to the liquid fund, we proceed with the transaction to reinvest in the recommended funds for your portfolio. This approach also ensures that debits are not initiated when funds have not yet reached your bank account, preventing unnecessary charges.

title: I'm currently investing with Scripbox through my savings account, but I've recently become an NRI. Could you assist me in updating my resident status from Resident Indian (RI) to Non-Resident Indian (NRI)?
content:

Sorry! nothing found for
I'm currently investing with Scripbox through my savings account, but I've recently become an NRI. Could you assist me in updating my resident status from Resident Indian (RI) to Non-Resident Indian (NRI)?
Modified on Fri, 6 Mar at 10:06 AM
Any resident status change process involves four steps.
1. KYC Modification
2. Creation of a new account
3. Updating the new TAX status in the existing folios
4. Movement of Folios to the new account
For updating the KYC (7-10 working days) (Scanned and Physical Copies):
Self-signed PAN
Self-signed Passport (first and last page)
Self-signed Overseas address proof (accepted proofs are mentioned below)
International Driver’s licence
Utility bills (internet/water/electricity)
KYC form: Kindly affix your passport-size photo, sign across the same and also sign on the first and second page in the given slot (Applicant E sign and Applicant Wet signature). The details that are required to be filled in are contact number, email ID, and PAN details. Please do not fill in any other details, we will do what is needed according to the documents you will share with us. Attached is the KYC form).
FATCA forms (CAMS and KARVY): The forms are to be filled and signed along with the TIN details (required by the fund houses and the KYC registration agencies). (Attached)
Note:
The KYC modification process is offline and we would need the above-mentioned physical documents to be attested by a scheduled Indian bank (in India or overseas) or from a Notary Public or by the Indian Embassy and then couriered to our office address.
We request you share the coloured scanned copies of the documents for the KYC change via email for pre-verification. Post confirmation that documents are good to proceed, they are required to be couriered to our office address.
Creation of New Account (within 2 hours from the time of completion of sign-up):
Once the KYC is registered as an NRI, request you to create a new Scripbox account by adding yourself as a family member in your Scripbox account, entering the new NRO bank account details & with the Resident status as NRI.
Steps to add yourself as a family member
Once the account is active, we recommend you not start the investments in your new NRI account until the entire process is completed. This is to avoid any investments being rejected due to Multiple Tax Status at the folios.
For updating the folios with the new Tax status (10 working days)
(Scanned and Physical Copies
):
New bank proof:
Cancelled cheque with name printed for NRO bank account or Latest bank statement with the recent three months transactions sheet along with banker's attestation by Bank Manager or above (round seal, signature, designation, name, and employee code)
Old Bank Proof:
Cancelled cheque with name printed for Savings bank account (
details as per registered bank account with Scripbox)
or Latest bank statement with
the recent three months transactions sheet
along with banker's attestation
by Bank Manager or above
(round seal, signature, designation, name, and employee code)
Signed Resident change form (form will be shared later once the KYC is approved as NRI and NRI account is active)
Movement of folios (1 working day):
Once the tax status is updated in the folios, we will move the folios from the Resident-Indian account to the new NRI account.
If we need any additional documents, we shall let you know accordingly. The change of resident status usually takes 4 weeks from the time we have received the complete set of documents.
Office address:
Scripbox Wealth Managers Pvt Ltd, Wilshire III by MFAR, 5th Floor, 492, Hobli, RHB Colony, Mahadevapura, Bengaluru, Karnataka- 560048

Sorry! nothing found for
How can I place a withdrawal request using my Scripbox?
Modified on Mon, 21 Oct, 2024 at 10:17 AM
You can follow the below steps to place the withdrawal request using your Scripbox:
1. Login in to your Scripbox account
2. Mutual Funds Tab - Click on Withdraw
3. Click on the Investor Name - Click on Continue
4. Click on the Plan from where you are looking to withdraw - You can either click on Fund Recommendations or Choose My Funds as per your preference.
If you are looking to place the withdrawal request for all the units, use the below steps :
1. Click on the Choose My funds
2. Select the fund
3. Click on Withdraw from this fund
4. Click on the second option (withdraw all units in this fund)
5. Click Continue
6. Withdraw
7. Confirm the withdrawal
8. Authenticate with OTP (via mobile or email)
If you are looking to place the withdrawal request for a particular fund, use the below steps :
1. Click on the Choose My funds
2. Click on Long-Term Portfolio - Click the Plan which you are looking to Place the withdrawal
3. Click on the fund - Click on Withdraw from this Fund
4. You will get 3 options - Choose to enter the customer amount (which should be greater than Rs. 500), withdraw all units in this fund and Withdraw long term gains only
5. Click Continue
6. Withdraw
7. Confirm the withdrawal
8. Authenticate with OTP (via mobile or email)

How do I set Notification preferences?
Modified on Thu, 14 Nov, 2024 at  2:05 PM
You can set your notification preferences on Scripbox by following the below steps:
1. Log in to your Scripbox account
2. Click on Profile tab > Notifications Preferences under profile settings
3. You can set the preferences for email / push / Whatsapp / SMS notifications
You can opt out from most of the communication we send, except transactional, if you wish – although this may lead to a sub-par experience for you. However, we are very mindful of the communication we send and ensure that we never spam our customers.
We do not share your email and phone number with any third-party sales companies. However, in order to enable you to invest, we do share your communication information with the mutual fund companies and SEBI-regulated KYC registration agencies (e.g. CAMS, Karvy, CDSL, NSDL). Please note the frequency and content of these messages is not within Scripbox’s control and you cannot unsubscribe from these.

Sorry! nothing found for
Your recommended funds of last year are no longer recommended this year. Why?
Modified on Thu, 14 Nov, 2024 at  5:14 PM
The concept of "best funds" is not static as various factors such as market fluctuations, mutual fund company performance, fund manager expertise, and consistency influence the score of each fund. Therefore, it is crucial to periodically review the recommended funds. Our recommendation list is refreshed annually, usually in April. Quarterly portfolio reviews ensure that you continue to invest in the right funds for your financial goals.

What are the parameters based on which you evaluated my mutual fund holdings?
Modified on Thu, 14 Nov, 2024 at  5:25 PM
Scripbox Portfolio Health assesses your portfolio based on 9 critical parameters. These include
Credit quality
We assess if the debt holdings are creditworthy.
Interest rate sensitivity
We assess if the debt funds in your family portfolio reacted well to the interest rate sensitivity.
Performance
We assess the consistency of all your mutual fund holdings.
Fund size
We check if there are any mutual funds in your family portfolio which has a low AUM size compared to the peers within the category.
Protect during market lows
We assess the funds on their performance during market downturns.
Participation in market highs
We check how the fund performed when the market rallied.
Optimization potential
We identify potential ways to optimize your portfolio.
Portfolio Clutter
We check if you have negligible investments in funds that don't add much value to your portfolio but only add clutter.
Concentration risk
We check if you have invested a large portion only in one fund.

How can I set up a SIP/One Time Instruction in my Scripbox account?
Modified on Mon, 21 Oct, 2024 at 10:15 AM
You can follow the below steps to set up a SIP/One Time Instruction in your Scripbox account:
Setting up an SIP
1. Login in to your Scripbox account
2. Mutual Funds Tab - Click on Invest
3. You can click on the Investment Plan - You either Click on View Scripbox Recommendation or I want to choose on my own
4. Click on SIP - Enter the Investment amount - Click on Continue
5. You can customise the fund allocation as per your preference by clicking on Customise where you can change the fund or amount allocated.
6. Click on Invest - Set up a SIP date
7. Click on Proceed to Payment
8. You will get three options to make the payment which are UPI, Net Banking and Bank Mandate. Click on the preferred mode of payment to invest - Click on Continue
9. Verification required - You will be receiving an OTP to place the order via SMS and Email - Click on either option to proceed further.
10. Enter the OTP and continue with the payment
Setting up a One Time Instruction
1. Login in to your Scripbox account
2. Mutual Funds Tab - Click on Invest
3. You can click on the Investment Plan - You either Click on View Scripbox Recommendation or I want to choose on my own
4. Click on One-time instruction - Enter the Investment amount - Click on Continue
5. You can customise the fund allocation as per your preference by clicking on Customise where you can change the fund or amount allocated.
6. Click on Invest
7. Click on Proceed to Payment
8. You will get three options to make the payment which are UPI, Net Banking and Bank Mandate. Click on the preferred mode of payment to invest - Click on Continue
9. Verification required - You will be receiving an OTP to place the order via SMS and Email - Click on either option to proceed further.
10. Enter the OTP and continue with the payment

title: I want to update/change the bank account details registered to my investments, how can I do this?
content:
All
Articles
Recent Searches
Clear all
No recent searches
Popular Articles
Articles
View all
Topics
View all
Tickets
View all
Sorry! nothing found for
I want to update/change the bank account details registered to my investments, how can I do this?
Modified on Fri, 6 Mar at 10:11 AM
The bank change process involves three steps:
1. Raise the request for bank change from your Scripbox account
2. Updating the new bank account details in the existing folios
3. Updating the new bank account details  in the Scripbox records
Steps to raise the bank change request via the app:
a. Login into your Scripbox account
b. Click on Profile (towards the right-hand side corner on the top of the home page)
c. Click on the investor name - Under Investments - Click on Scripbox - Click on Change next to your Bank account
d. Edit the bank account details and Click on Update Changes
For updating the new bank account details at the folios (7-10 working days) (Scanned Copies):
Once you raise the bank change request, you are required to share the below-mentioned documents in scanned copies to update your new bank account details in your Scripbox account as well as at the folios in which you have been investing :
a. Bank Change letter with your wet signature (you will receive the letter once the request is raised)
b. Old bank proof: cancelled cheque with name printed or 3 months bank statement with self and banker's attestation or passbook with 3 months transaction sheet (self and banker's attestation by bank manager or above with round s
eal, signature, designation, name, and employee code
is mandatory)
c. New bank proof: cancelled cheque with name printed or latest 3 months bank statement with self and banker's attestation or passbook with latest 3 months transaction sheet (self and banker's attestation by bank manager or above with round s
eal, signature, designation, name, and employee code
is mandatory)
d. Self-signed PAN card copy.
For updating the new bank account details in your Scripbox account (1 working day):
a.Once the new bank account details are updated at the folios, the new bank account details will be updated in the Scripbox records.
Note: When the bank change request is processed, you will not be able to invest or withdraw. This is to ensure that the wrong bank account does not get used.
Whenever any change of bank details request is received simultaneously with or just prior to the submission of a redemption request, mutual funds allow a cooling period of ten days as a matter of precaution against unauthorised/fraudulent transactions. You will be able to place a redemption request once the cooling period is over.

title: I have recently relocated to India and would like to update my resident status from Non-Resident Indian (NRI) to Resident Indian (RI). Could you please assist me with this change?
content:
All
